title: Accessible Content Models, Landmarks, Headings, Links, Media, and Language · Transfer Build
summary: An independent documentation team receives a different content tree, language, route base, template, asset set, trust policy, hosting target, and accessibility need and transfers accessible content models, landmarks, headings, links, media, and language into a changed WCAG 2.2 content and template conformance gate. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of WCAG 2.2 content and template conformance gate with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through pre…
objectives: Enforce document language, unique main, named navigation, logical heading outline, bypass mechanism, title, and meaningful sequence.
Require meaningful link text, current-location evidence, multiple ways to find pages, breadcrumb semantics, and non-color state.
Validate image purpose, alt decision, captions, transcripts, audio description needs, decorative handling, and accessible fallback.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Landmark Heading Model
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab publishing team handles case 7334 during a large technical reference incremental rebuild. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate generated page structure and content on WCAG evidence and human reader tasks; retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Landmark Heading Model.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for enforce document language, unique main, named navigation, logical heading outline, bypass mechanism, title, and meaningful sequence. For Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Landmark Heading Model, keep Landmark Heading Model connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

options.text: For commit to the mission model, render one trusted short page at the root, omit content authority, source spans, parser limits, route base, URL and asset reconciliation, contextual encoding, accessibility, clean-build comparison, deployment, rollback, recovery, and production transfer, then accept plausible HTML as publication proof. State the Landmark Heading Model preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.
Valid HTML guarantees an understandable landmark and heading structure. That claim fails the commit to the mission model evidence boundary for Landmark Heading Model.

stimulus.title: Accessibility Evidence case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A lab publishing team handles case 8953 during a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate generated page structure and content on WCAG evidence and human reader tasks; retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Competency probe: Combine structural checks, keyboard and zoom review, screen-reader tasks, content review, changed routes, and human ownership.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: An automated accessibility scanner can certify the whole static site.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Combine structural checks, keyboard and zoom review, screen-reader tasks, content review, changed routes, and human ownership. Artifact under review: WCAG 2.2 content and template conformance gate.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Accessibility Evidence, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Accessibility Evidence record says WCAG 2.2 content and template conformance gate looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the audit the initial evidence packet evidence boundary for Accessibility Evidence.

For audit the initial evidence packet, render one trusted short page at the root, omit content authority, source spans, parser limits, route base, URL and asset reconciliation, contextual encoding, accessibility, clean-build comparison, deployment, rollback, recovery, and production transfer, then accept plausible HTML as publication proof. This does not satisfy Accessibility Evidence for WCAG 2.2 content and template conformance gate.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-accessibility-evidence-19daas
# Competency: ssg-accessibility-evidence.
# Case 2992: a multilingual public-service handbook migration.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence.
def evidence_ssg_accessibility_evidence_19daas(pages: int = 8, checked_pages: int = 8, structural_findings: int = 0, media_decisions_missing: int = 0, human_tasks_passed: int = 4) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_19daas = "19daas-saad91-2992"
    assert evidence_variant_19daas
    evidence_axes_19daas = (
        "reader19daas",
        "source2992",
        "parsesaad91",
        "route19daas2992",
        "artifact299219daas",
        "recoverysaad912992",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_19daas)) == 6
    if pages < 1 or checked_pages < 0:
        return False, checked_pages, "page-count"
    if checked_pages != pages:
        return False, checked_pages, "accessibility-coverage"
    if structural_findings > 0 or media_decisions_missing > 0:
        return False, structural_findings + media_decisions_missing, "accessibility-finding"
    if human_tasks_passed < 3:
        return False, human_tasks_passed, "human-evidence"
    assert checked_pages == pages
    return True, human_tasks_passed, "accessible-content-model"
